title: PARACITY Manual Coffee Bean Grinder Stainless Steel Hand Mill Ceramic Burr for Aeropress, Drip Coffee, Espresso, French Press, Turkish Brew, for gift
reviews_5s:
I really like this hand crank coffee grinder, it has a dial to adjust how coarse you want your grounds to be, and is relatively simple to assemble, use, and clean. It does take a long time to grind the coffee, but that's expected as it only can go as fast as your arms can! LolI enjoy making coffee in my sailboat and try to keep the use of power down. I bought the PARACITY Manual Coffee Bean Grinder because whole beans keep better than ground coffee, this grinder uses no power, and the price was great. I was apprehensive because of the price but the grinder does everything I would want from a grinder. The texture is easily adjustable, it has plenty of capacity to make enough grounds for my normal 30 oz tumbler of coffee, and it grinds easily. I cannot speak to reliability as I've only had the grinder for a few days but I am very happy with this purchase so far. The product could be improved with some 1/4 or 1/8 turn threads to hold the bottom grounds container and the lid on and maybe the same for the handle but I have had no trouble with any of it staying together in use as is.This grinder is easy to use and clean. It breaks down to make it simple to store in my cabinet, drawer, or backpack.I will carry this in our travel trailer. It is important to have a quiet coffee grinder as there is no door, only a curtain between the kitchen area and the sleeping area. I can grind fresh coffee without disturbing my wife. Now I can carry bean coffee and grind it fresh.

I tested it by doing a fine grind. The grounds were consistent in size and truly fine and not the mixed sizes I would get with a flat burr electric grinder. I think it will hold up if I take care to pad it well when traveling.

The only thing missing is a carry pouch. That would have been nice to have included. It isn't enough to take away a star though.Disregard all CONS. Add an additional 1/4 inch (or 6 mm) locknut under the set screw below the burr. This will lick the burr in place and deliver consistent grind time after time.
Insert the square end of a 1/4 inch socket for 1/4 inch nut. This will fit a 1/4 inch hex adapter for your cheap cordless screwdriver to turn the burr and grind away. I can grind 20 gram beans to finest dust (espresso quality) in under a minute (approx 220 revolutions … ouch).
I have bought 3 ($9.99 each) so I never have to fiddle with any adjustments. One each for fine, medium, and coarse grind!
Beats the high end fancy grinder (80$ and up, with strapless grind, walnut wood knobs and hand polished exterior etc) into a silly pulp.
Invest in a bulb type air blower to clean the interiors which get coated with chaff and broken coffee beans.
I spray beans with about 1/2 ml of water before loading into the grinder. Eliminates static problem.
I also store my beans in dark glass under vacuum, this may sound complicated but it is not.
Coffee is serious business (more serious as well as useful and healthy then wine) please avoid cutting corners.I can not say if this is a good coffee grinder because I use it for Peppercorns. For this, it functions beautifully and has sense its first use. The bottom storage is excellent for when you need a large amount of pepper, like for holiday dinners or cook-outs. The grind adjustment functions well from fine to coarse, so it is always useful.I was really grateful to find this coffee grinder for $8.99, when I'd expected to have to spend $18.99. It's only the second day using it, but it works well. I had a momentary scare ("Is it really breaking down on the second use!? Shouldn't have bought the cheapest"), then realized to hold onto the base of the adjustable wheel while turning it counterclockwise, so it won't be looser than functioning. It might be nice if the lid on the grinder also fit as a lid for the well of grounds.I love the grinder. It works quite well. However, the person who wrote the directions should be fired. They were incomprehensible, and there were no diagrams to make it clear how to adjust the setting for what level of grind you wanted. The directions make it sound like you control settings by the direction you turn the crank. This is not the case.bYou can change the grind by turning the little black dial inside. Clockwise makes it fine; counter clockwise makes it coarse.works.Me gustó
reviews_1s:
This grinder broke immediately. I had it for one week, and it broke while I was camping. I fixed one problem with a metal nut. Then something else broke beyond repair, though I tried to repair with super glue. Would love to contact Paricity customer service about a refund or replacement, but I can't find a way to contact this company.Does handheld coffee grinder is only convenient for campers or people who want small cups of coffee. It does have various texture options. But you have to hand crank this and it took me 10 to 15 minutes between me and my husband to get coffee grounds out of it and that was with a drill attached to it. The design of this product is ignorant. The stainless steel is brushed metal and it does not feel good when you're putting the lid on. The handle comes off way too easy. It is compact. And it doesn't make much noise. It does make your coffee taste good. But if you want quick coffee the speed of this product is not for you.It came missing the nut on top. I figured I'd be able to find one somewhere, but never did. I get tired of it falling over and spilling all the peppercorns, but it grinds okay.
In a perfect world, I'd buy another one with the top nut, but life's not perfect.
Update: finally, the grinder handle went missing. Now it is worthless. I guess it did not ever come with a locking nut for the top- so now I'll buy a different kind.Build quality was poor.
- The grinder was not centered correctly, and either the shaft was bent, or the ceramic bur was not concentric. While one side, both ceramic elements would touch, the other side had a gap, and if you spun the crank the spacing was not consistent. Photo attached shows this issue.
- This second point is minor, and if this was the only issue kept it. The two halves felt loose, I would be worried about it falling out while grinding.

Other reviews seemed like this was fine, so either their quality has declined, or its a roll of the dice to see if you get a good or bad one. It may be worth getting a more expensive one to avoid these problems.

Returned for refund.Play Video
I would give this 0 stars, if possible. The shaft of the grinder is visibly bent, as shown in the photo (marker added for comparison,) which makes it impossible to use. The way it was packaged hid the defect from casual inspection, making me suspect that I was deliberately and fraudulently sold a defective item, since this could not have occurred during shipment. Had I gifted this as intended, the bent shaft would have caused the porcelain grinder to splinter and break, leaving sharp splinters of porcelain in the grounds. This is incredibly hazardous in a cold brew or French press.The bottom piece that attaches to the shaft is plastic. It broke before 3 months. I've been using these type of grinders for over 7 years. They usually last over a year. So bummed!Broke on first use.
I put the beans in and it immediately bound up on some coffee beans, emptied, refilled with less beans just to see and it immediately became stuck again. I removed the beans and put them back in again and when I tried to grind it got stuck for a moment then spun freely as the cheap plastic that held the ceramic grind stone together stripped out making this thing useless.Center rod around which everything rotates is slightly bent so the grinder touches itself on one half and is wide open on the other, as other reviewers have experienced. If your preferred grind size is half coarse and half powder, this product is for you!Handle only lasted 2 grinds due to poor quality. Pentagon shaped hole lost shape after 2 grinds and now just fre spins on rod. Totally worthless.This product was fine for a couple of months, even though it's made of cheap materials. However, after 7 months the plastic inside started falling apart and which means there was plastic being ground into my coffee. Additionally, the whole grinder got loose so every time I grind, the whole mechanism rotates and nothing gets ground. I've tried taking it apart, cleaning it, pushing harder but it takes 4x as long to ground now because the grinder keeps slipping. Terrible do not recommend. Spend a little more and save in the long run.
